ogTitle: Teddywaddy West vs Teesdale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Teddywaddy West and Teesdale.
heading: Teddywaddy West vs Teesdale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Teddywaddy West, Victoria and Teesdale, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Teesdale has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Teddywaddy West (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Teddywaddy West or Teesdale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Teddywaddy West has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Teesdale scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Teddywaddy West scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Teesdale scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Teddywaddy West or Teesdale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Teesdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
